title: A Fashion Founder Steps Down
summary: Stefano Gabbana, who started the fashion brand Dolce & Gabbana with his friend Domenico Dolce in 1985, has stopped being the chair of the company. He will still help design clothes with Domenico. The company is facing money problems with a lot of debt, around 450 million euros, because people are spending less on luxury items, especially in China. Alfonso Dolce, Domenico's brother, is now the new chair. The brand is trying new things like making furniture and vases, but it needs help to manage its finances better. Experts say the company might get a new partner to fix the money issues.

keywords.explanation: money that a company owes to others
the person who leads a company's meetings
a company that makes and sells clothes and accessories
expensive things like fancy clothes or jewelry
someone who works with others in a business

questions.question: Who stepped down as chair of Dolce & Gabbana?
What year was Dolce & Gabbana founded?
How much debt does the company have?
Who is the new chair of the company?
What will Stefano Gabbana still do at the company?
Why is the company having money problems?
options: People are spending less on luxury items
questions.correct_answer: People are spending less on luxury items

background_read: Dolce & Gabbana is a famous fashion brand from Italy that makes expensive clothes and accessories. It was started by two friends, Domenico Dolce and Stefano Gabbana, in 1985. The brand is known for its stylish designs and is popular with celebrities. Companies can have money problems if they owe a lot of debt, which means they borrowed money and need to pay it back. When people buy fewer luxury items, it can hurt a company's sales. Sometimes, companies change leaders or find new partners to help with financial issues.
Article_Structure: WHO: Stefano Gabbana, co-founder of Dolce & Gabbana
WHAT: He stepped down as chair but will keep designing clothes
WHERE: The company is based in Milan, Italy
WHY: The company has debt and needs financial help due to less spending on luxury items

perspectives.description: The company is struggling with debt and needs to find ways to manage its money better.
